response.justification: Bogdan brings 15+ years of demonstrated fullstack engineering across Node.js, TypeScript, React, and multiple database technologies, far exceeding the 6+ year threshold. The explicit AI agent infrastructure work at re:cinq — including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — is a direct and rare match for a listed requirement. Testing culture is deep and evidenced by open-source testing tooling and teaching. The main gaps are the absence of any Nest.js mention (the primary backend framework in the stack), no explicit citation of Jest, Vitest, or Playwright by name, and no Kotlin experience despite it appearing as a secondary requirement. These gaps warrant probing in interview but do not disqualify an otherwise strong profile.
strengths: Extensive Node.js and TypeScript fullstack experience across multiple production roles at scale (millions of requests/s at Dunnhumby, millions of mailings at Optilyz)
Direct AI agent infrastructure experience at re:cinq including MCP code generator frameworks and agentic workflow tooling — an explicit job requirement rarely matched
Deep testing culture: practiced TDD across roles, authored open-source D-language testing libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and ran public TDD/testing workshops
concerns: Nest.js, the primary backend framework in the required stack, is not mentioned anywhere in the resume
Jest, Vitest, and Playwright are not cited by name; testing expertise is inferred from TDD practices and custom tooling rather than demonstrated with the exact frameworks listed
Kotlin is absent from the skills list; only Java appears, and in roles from 2016–2018 rather than recent work
